description: BIS Standard for Hair Oils (Type 1, 2, and 3). Includes analytical characteristics, heavy metal limits, and moisture checks.

rules.name: Mercury Ban
rules.description: Mercury and its compounds are strictly banned in cosmetics and hair oils under BIS and CDSCO.

rules.name: Lead Limit
rules.description: Lead (Pb) must not exceed 20 ppm in hair oils as per BIS heavy metal guidelines.

rules.name: Arsenic Limit
rules.description: Arsenic (As) must not exceed 2 ppm (2 mg/kg) in cosmetic hair oils.

rules.name: Heavy Metals Cumulative Limit
rules.description: Cumulative sum of Lead + Arsenic must not exceed 22 ppm.

rules.name: Moisture Content
rules.description: Moisture test limits must not exceed 5.5% as per BIS IS 7123:2019 to prevent rancidity.

rules.name: Acid Value
rules.description: Acid value for Type 1 and Type 3 hair oils shall not be more than 1.0, to prevent irritation and ensure oil stability.

rules.name: Preservatives Limit
rules.description: Paraben-based preservatives should not exceed 0.4% in hair oils.

rules.name: Total Fragrance Limit
rules.description: Total fragrant compounds should generally not exceed 1.0% in hair oils.

rules.name: Glycerin Limit
rules.description: While glycerin helps with moisture, concentrations above 5% in hair oil can cause sticky buildup and dirt attraction.

rules.name: Base Oil Content (Minimum)
rules.description: Base oil (Coconut, Olive, Mineral, or blended vegetable oils) must constitute at least 85% of the total formulation for Type 1 and Type 3 hair oils (BIS IS 7123:2019).

rules.name: Herbal Extracts Limit
rules.description: Herbal/botanical extracts should not exceed 2% of total formulation — excessive concentrations risk allergic reactions and formulation instability.

rules.name: Almond Oil Limit
rules.description: Secondary emollient oils like almond oil should not exceed 10% of the total hair oil formulation.
